frameworks.name: natural scene statistics
frameworks.one_line: direct measurement of statistical regularities in natural environments shapes perceptual theory

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: perceptual systems designed by evolution and experience to match environmental statistics

core_question: How can direct measurement of statistical regularities in natural scenes advance our understanding of visual perception?
key_assumptions: perceptual systems shaped by evolution and experience to match task demands
natural environment statistics constrain optimal perceptual design
scene statistics are tractably measurable with existing techniques
manipulations: types of natural scene statistics measured (over a 20-year span)
measurement techniques applied to natural scenes
key_findings: 20 years of natural scene statistics measurements yielded a coherent body of regularities
multiple distinct kinds of scene statistics can be quantified with existing tools
scene-statistics findings have generated testable hypotheses, models, and experiments about vision
interpretations: natural scene statistics offers a principled route to understanding visual perception
linking environmental regularities to perceptual mechanism is a productive research program
